project_constraints.charset_note: Gate FALHOU: a fonte do jogo não tem diacríticos — pangrama pt-BR renderiza como '@' (evidência in-game: artifacts/char1.png, char2.png). Estratégia adotada: TRANSLITERAÇÃO na gravação (acento→ASCII) no reinsert.py; a tradução canônica mantém os acentos. Ver decision_log.md.

glossary_subset.notes: Ave Hororon gigante; montaria/pet da Rulutieh.
Unidade militar do Ukon -> 'Coorte do Ukon'. Traduzir 'Cohort'->'Coorte' consistente.
Rótulo de falante (Kuon antes do nome). Onde o budget não permite (órfão 0xe1da), usar 'Moça'.
Nome dado ao protagonista por Kuon em 11_02 (0x12668). reveal_timing: cena 11_02. Antes disso ele é sem nome.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

Erudito/escriba/mago comico e franzino do grupo do Ukon (cap.12); magia de fogo com cantico+danca longos. Fala em registro ARCAICO e comico (pleaseth/mine own; gag de cicio s->sh). Gruda no Haku como 'benfeitor'.
Criminoso capturado; preso com ajuda de Nosuri e Ougi.
Fora-da-lei, 'aliada da justica'. Irma de Ougi. Trabalhou com Oshtor/Haku.
Princesa de Kujyuri, filha de Ozen. Timida; gosta de BL; desenha e cozinha bem. Anda com a ave Cocopo. Fonte: corpus cap.13 + wiki.

Guerreiro espirituoso e cativante que faz amizade com Haku ao ve-lo consertar o moinho (cap.12); lidera a 'Coorte do Ukon'. Maroro o trata por 'Mestre Ukon'. SPOILER: identidade verdadeira e reveal de arco posterior — NAO antecipar; no cap.12 e um personagem por si so. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

lines: Registro: cotidiano gentil; cuidadora.
Características: acolhe sem pressionar ("Apenas relaxe..."); curiosa mas paciente; calor humano.
Red flags: soar fria, clínica ou impaciente; formalidade excessiva.
Haku (protagonista, narração 1ª pessoa) — `voice_criticality: high`. Predomina nas 2 cenas:
Registro: enigmático, profético, frio.
Características: sentenças curtas e definitivas; duplo sentido ("um mundo totalmente novo te espera"); ambiguidade ameaçadora.
Red flags: soar caloroso/paternal; resolver a ambiguidade (a fala deve permanecer dúbia).
Registro: erudito cômico, pomposo e arcaico; bajulador do Ukon; alívio cômico.
Características: fala arcaica/empolada (equivalente a "vós/peço-vos/meus olhos não me enganam") + gag de cicio (s→sh, ex.: "SHEEKETH"/"shir"); melodramático; gruda no Haku como "benfeitor".
Red flags: perder o arcaísmo ou o cicio (são a piada); soar moderno/neutro; explicar a comédia. Manter o contraste entre a pompa do vocabulário e a fragilidade do personagem.
Moznu — `voice_criticality: low`. Criminoso (antagonista menor); registro grosseiro.
Nosuri — `voice_criticality: medium`. Fora-da-lei atrevida e malandra; "aliada da justiça" irônica; oportunista. Registro coloquial/esperto.

Oshtor — `voice_criticality: high`. = Ukon até 13_08 (ver spoiler_ledger). Registro formal, nobre, comedido; General da Direita. Antes do reveal, traduzir como o mercenário "Ukon" (espirituoso, informal) — NÃO antecipar a pompa de general
Ougi — `voice_criticality: low`. Irmão da Nosuri; pragmático, parceria com a irmã.
Registro: confuso, desorientado, semi-consciente.
Características: frases quebradas por reticências; perguntas curtas ("Quem... é você...?"); pouca pontuação forte.
Red flags: falas fluentes/articuladas demais; perder o tom de torpor; pontuação "limpa" que apaga a fragmentação.
Rulutieh — `voice_criticality: medium`. Princesa tímida e gentil; hesitante (gagueja quando nervosa: "P-Princesa..."), educada, se anima ao falar de arte/BL. Não soar arrogante apesar de princesa.
Registro: guerreiro carismático, espirituoso, caloroso e informal; líder nato que trata Haku como um irmão mais novo.
Características: desembaraçado, brincalhão, generoso; fala direta e cativante; autoridade leve (lidera a Coorte) sem arrogância.
Red flags: soar rígido/formal/militar demais (a graça é justamente o contraste com a patente); frieza. SPOILER: não deixar a tradução insinuar a identidade verdadeira — no cap.12 ele é só "Ukon".

decisions.title: Opcode de RÓTULO DE FALANTE `53 00` + reconcile de speaker (data-driven)
decisions.summary: **Problema:** in-game, o rótulo de falante aparecia em **inglês** ("Girl") mesmo com a tradução ("Garota") aprovada e gravada. RE: o nome do falante usa um **2º opcode de ponteiro, `53 00`** (mesmo formato file-relativo do `50 00` de diálogo), que o conector ignorava. Resultado: "Girl"→"Garota"

decisions.title: Gate de charset pt-BR — método e veredito
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: likely` e exigir confirmação in-game antes de produção. **Alternativas consideradas:** - Confirmar por presença no texto-fonte — **insuficiente**: o fonte é inglês e quase não usa acentos (só `õ` e `À` aparecem em texto real).

decisions.title: Anomalia 0x33f9 — texto PT/EN corrompido na fonte
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar a linha `0x33f9` como anomalia de fonte (não traduzir em cima do lixo; tratar como linha de sistema reescrita). **Razão:** A string original já vem misturada PT/EN e truncada ("...SISTEMAS AM . RESTARTING...") no próprio jogo. Não é erro de extração. O conector deve sinali

decisions.title: Charset — transliteração na gravação (gate FALHOU)
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: false` e **transliterar** (acento → ASCII) na gravação do binário. A tradução canônica (`approved_translations.csv`, `translation_plan.json`) **mantém os acentos** (correta para QA/revisão); apenas os bytes escritos no jogo são dobrados para ASCI

decisions.title: Escopo cognitivo — 75 → 1025 linhas (cenas 11_01 + 11_02); reveal de Haku in-corpus
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Re-rodar o pipeline completo em escala (cenas 11_01 + 11_02 = 1025 linhas). Novos termos canônicos: **Kuon** (nome revelado em 0x108db), **Haku** (nome dado ao protagonista em 0x12668 — reveal agora **dentro do corpus**), **Tatari** (criatura imortal), **aperyu** (vestimenta), **

decisions.title: CORREÇÃO CRÍTICA — ponteiros são FILE-RELATIVOS, não absolutos
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Ao investigar o "opcode de início de bloco", descobri que **`50 00`+uint32 é um offset RELATIVO ao início do arquivo (Pack)**, não absoluto. Endereço da string = `file_start_do_site + uint32`. Prova: dos ~47k sites, **42.101** só apontam para string como file-relativos vs **63**

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: Well, guess the sun still rises no matter where\n
tm_voice.target: Enfim, o sol nasce em qualquer lugar, pelo visto\n

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: I am. Still... What am I supposed to do now...?
tm_voice.target: Pois é. Mesmo assim... O que é que eu faço agora...?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, you there! Could you spare a moment?
tm_voice.target: Ei, você aí! Pode me dar um momento?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, I'm sorry for bothering you, but could I ask\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: Master Ukon! It pleaseth my heart to report my\n
tm_voice.target: Mestre Ukon! É com grande satisfação que reporto que meus\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: belongings lay duly unpack'd, and await porters.
tm_voice.target: meus pertences estão desfeitos e aguardam os carregadores.

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: I am VERY tired, sir. Naught more now do I desire\n
tm_voice.target: Estou MUITO cansado, senhor. Nada mais desejo agora\n

tm_voice.speaker: Ukon
tm_voice.source: Really, Maroro? Seems like you get tired quicker\n
tm_voice.target: É sério, Maroro? Parece que você se cansa mais rápido\n

tm_voice.speaker: Homem
tm_voice.source: The way you were carrying on, you got us all\n
tm_voice.target: Do jeito que você estava, nos deixou todos\n

tm_voice.speaker: Rulutieh
tm_voice.source: That's a relief... Come on, Cocopo. We'll just be\n
tm_voice.target: Ainda bem... Vamos, Cocopo. Só estamos\n

spoiler_guards.fact: Ukon e na verdade Oshtor, o General da Direita / Imperial Guard do Mikado. Reconhecido publicamente em ch_13_08 (0x77ef9, 'Could that be... Sir Oshtor?').
spoiler_guards.guard: ANTES de 13_08: trate Ukon como mercenario espirituoso por si so. NAO insinue patente, nobreza, vinculo imperial nem o nome 'Oshtor'. Preserve a ambiguidade do original (que usa 'Ukon').

spoiler_guards.fact: As identidades das figuras de memoria/sonho ('Woman'/'Man', 'Mestre').
spoiler_guards.guard: Use rotulos genericos (Mulher/Homem/Mestre). NAO resolva quem sao nem o vinculo com Haku. Preserve o tom enigmatico. (Obs.: 'Master Ukon' do Maroro NAO e isto — e so o honorifico do Ukon.)

lines.source: The caravan continues on its slow, inexorable roll\n
up the mountain path...
I can tell the road here was paved at SOME point,\n
but now, only a scattered few flat stones remain.
Trees have taken root in the days since, roots\n
snaking under the surface and breaking up the\n
path.
So this is what Ukon meant when he said the road\n
would be rough...
The snowmelt has sunken into the dirt, too,\n
forcing everyone to trudge through the thick mud.
Mysterious duo right
Mysterious duo left
*Poke-poke-poke*...
*Squish-squish-squish*...
As usual, the Mystery Twins are close at hand.
This is... Ah, forget it.
No matter what I say, it's not like they're gonna\n
reply. It's pointless to even worry about it.
...Man, though. It's getting dark.
The sun SHOULD be at high noon, but the thick\n
canopy growth above us is blocking it out\n
effectively.
Ne'ertofore have I breathed so foul an air as that\n
which chilleth my very bones in this place...
Maroro mutters uneasily as he shoots furtive\n
glances about, peering into the trees.
It calleth forward a memory most unseemly, of a\n
darkling scroll that fearful once I read.
'Twas a tale of a man a-wandering in a wood like\n
this one, whence he met a woman of beauty quite\n
unmatch'd.
But lo, 'neath this woman's mask of deception lay\n
a nugwisomkami! That which feasteth upon the flesh\n
of wayfarers.
"Nugwisomkami?" So, what, it's some kind of\n
phantom or ghost or something...?
Doth the tale frighten thee not, Master Haku?
Not... really, no.
I-I see...
Not only is it kind of a generic story, but a\n
short summary isn't exactly gonna strike fear\n
into my heart.
Hm?
A figure on the road ahead catches my gaze...
Someone unfamiliar stands at the side of the road,\n
waving both arms in an effort to flag us down.
Next to them, a wagon much like one of our own has\n
tilted and fallen, blocking the road.
Ukon calls a halt, and a nervous vibe ripples through\n
the caravan, the prospect of bandits very real.
Girl
I'm so sorry, but could you help me out?
The young girl next to the wagon approaches us\n
with an apologetic smile.
What can we do for you, miss?
My cart's wheels are stuck in a ditch, and I can't\n
get it out on my own.
My companions went for help, but at this rate, the\n
sun's going to set before they come back...
I'm awfully sorry, but could I get your help\n
pushing the cart free?
Quite a bind you're in, huh. All right, men, let's\n
help her out!
Ukon's Cohort
Sir!
Leave it to us!
Several men quickly gather at Ukon's call.
They seem a little eager to please because it's a\n
woman--especially one with a pretty face. Figures.
Hey, Maroro. Is that your nugwisomkami?
How now?
The story you just told me. She gonna eat us up\n
while our guard is down?
G-Gadzooks, Master Haku, make not such jests!\n
'Tis no laughing matter!
Oh, come on, I'm not being serious. I just asked\n
if it looks like her.
I mean--she's cute, but I wouldn't call her a\n
woman of "beauty unmatch'd" or whatever you said.
O-One supposeth as much...
She possesseth some charm, but no. Milady is not\n
such a beauty as the fell creatures might\n
impersonate.
Forsooth, let us be thankful for thy homeliness...
Hm? Something wrong, miss?
No, not really...
You're very kind. If I may, can I make one more\n
request of you?
Sure, let's hear it.
Mm, put down your weapons and leave all your\n
other belongings behind.
Huh?
Put down your weapons, and leave your belongings\n
behind.
*FWOOSH*
In the blink of an eye, the dropcloth covering her\n
cart's cargo rips away, revealing armed men.
Ukon's men who had approached the caravan to help\n
lift it are quickly subdued and pinned down...
Ah--!
Wh--!
Don't move.
Faster than Ukon can reach for his blade, the girl\n
raises her hand...
At her signal, more men with bows and nocked\n
arrows materialize from the trees, aimed right\n
at us.
Yeek! Wh-Wh-What manner of--
We're surrounded!
Their numbers are only half ours, but every one of\n
them has his bowstring drawn and readied.
We'll have to play this very, very carefully.
M-Master Haku...!
Maroro begins visibly trembling at the sudden\n
development.
Both he and the twins are-- Wait, where did the\n
twins go?
...Vanished again, of course.
Hm. Not bad.
Ukon remains straight-backed and fearless, but his\n
voice is audibly stiff.
Worry not. We're merely after your cargo. Your\n
lives are only in danger if you decide to be\n
idiots.
Or... perhaps you'll resist, and put those two on\n
the bird at risk?
Is she talking about... Rulutieh and Kuon?
If they loose those arrows, the girls would be\n
first to fall. There's nowhere to take cover.
A-Ah... Uhm...
I look over to see Kuon protectively wrapping her\n
arms around Rulutieh, who's gone pale with fear.
...Guess there's no choice, then.
Ukon takes his katana from his waist and tosses it\n
at the woman's feet.
You guys do the same. Don't do anything rash.
I'm taking her at her word that she won't hurt us\n
if we stay quiet.
The ankuam all share looks, murmuring to each\n
other...
The reality of their situation sinks in, and one\n
by one, they lay down their arms.
You there. You too.
Maroro and I are the only ones not moving to put\n
down our weapons... We're drawing undue attention.
Looks like I have no choice...
I guess I can only ride out the storm and see what\n
happens, at this point.
I pull the fan from my sash and gently lay it on\n
the ground.
And the other one.
He doesn't have anything on him.
As Maroro dissolves into a panic, I step in and\n
speak for him.
O-Oh, Master Haku...!
Maroro takes my words as sticking up for him, and\n
he quivers, moved to tears.
...Really, I only did it so his flailing wouldn't\n
unnecessarily provoke them.
Very well. Men!
Brigand
Yes, ma'am!
At the woman's call, some of her bandits step\n
forward with ropes.
Hands behind your backs. Nice and slow-like.
There's no real point in resisting. I do as\n
instructed.
Didn't Ukon say no bandit would dare attack us\n
like this...?
And here we are. Being attacked. By bandits.
Glancing up the line to Ukon, I can see they've\n
also tied him up and forced him to sit.
At least these guys don't seem like the types to\n
do anything too rough...
Ha! Well done, Nosuri. A right impressive show,\n
this.
The sound of slow clapping fills the clearing...
A broad-shouldered, rugged man appears and gives\n
a vulgar smile, clapping as he approaches the\n
girl.
Despite his dramatic entrance, Nosuri only gives\n
him a sidelong glance, hardly paying attention.
The newcomer shrugs it off, looking over to me...
Or past me, more accurately, to Kuon and Rulutieh--\n
now dismounted and tied up. His smile widens by\n
several teeth.
Heh heh. Now, ain't this one a beauty? Lookers\n
like this are hard to come by, even in the city.
Yeah, that settles that. This girl is mine.
Bastard...
The woman--Nosuri, apparently--had promised they'd\n
only touch the cargo, but I guess that was a lie.
Hey, boys. Don't forget to search these idiots\n
while we've got 'em tied up. Take whatever you\n
come up with.
Bandits
Yeah!
At the broad-shouldered man's call, a bunch of\n
ragged ruffians--a new, separate group--approach\n
us...
This isn't good. How are we gonna get out of this\n
one...?
Sorry, but I'll pass. You aren't my type.
Hm...
Hmhm.
Oh, you're good. You've got spunk. I'm likin' you\n
even more, now!
Heh heh. You girls are mine, now, y'understand?\n
I'll be sure to give you plenty of "attention"\n
later.
Hey, boss, you'd better share!
You wish! I ain't sharing one little finger of\n
these lovelies.
Miss Kuon...
Pale with fear, Rulutieh huddles against Kuon,\n
shaking.
Hey, hey. Everything's going to be OK. We'll be\n
all right. I promise.
Well, someone's incredibly confident.
The situation's looking bleaker by the moment.\n
Does Kuon have some kind of secret plan up her\n
sleeve?
I'm sure Haku will find us a way out.
Sir Haku?
Me!?
Rulutieh follows Kuon's eyes, looking to me for\n
help.
Wh-What does she expect ME to do!?
Please stop looking at me like that. Those eyes\n
are plaguing me with guilt...
Moznu, enough. If you're going to be working with\n
the Nosuri Thieves from now on, you abide by our\n
rules, not yours.
The girl gives the man called Moznu a sharp look,\n
admonishing him.
We only take the cargo. Everything and everyONE\n
else is to remain unharmed.
I told you this before. You can't just do as you\n
please.
Ah, come on, don't be such a spoilsport! Live a\n
little.
You know you can just leave it aaall to me, babe.
Moznu swaggers over to Nosuri and throws an arm\n
around her shoulder, getting a little too close\n
for comfort.
I know you're just jealous 'cause I'm giving those\n
two attention. Why don't you come clean, already?
Be my sweetheart, yeah? It'd be an awful shame if\n
those great tits of yours never got the lovin'\n
they deserve.
As Moznu points meaningfully at Nosuri's chest,\n
she calmly seizes his finger and twists sharply.
*KRAK*
HRAAAGH!!
Moznu drops to the ground, clutching the offending\n
finger and writhing in pain.
D-Did she just--Fingers aren't... supposed to\n
bend that way...
Heh. Behave, little boy.
Nosuri says that line as though reciting it from\n
a script, then winks awkwardly.
How was that, Ougi? Did I act with all the style\n
and strength of a good woman?
Truly most impressive, dearest sister. Your\n
feminine charms dazzle, as ever.
Wh--?
A slender man appears next to me.
How was he hiding in plain sight? I didn't notice\n
him at all.
That's right. With this, I'm one step closer to\n
being the very model of a good woman!
Gh... guh... g-gah... What'd you do that for!?
He looks like he's in serious pain, thrashing\n
about as he howls at Nosuri.
How positively boorish. A good MAN simply\n
laughs and shrugs off a woman's jests.
How'm I supposed to shrug this off!? This shit\n
HURTS!
Oh, dear. This is exactly why you'll never\n
aspire to be a good man, Moznu.
There was once a foreign owlo who was thought to\n
be such a man, you know.
No matter how his jealous wife hit him or stabbed\n
him with her chopsticks, he grinned and bore it.
To weather such treatment with a smile and\n
acceptance--such is the duty of a good man!
...I'm finding a couple of things wrong with her\n
logic, here...
You, on the other hand, are a boor and a pig.
H-How dare--!?
The man grimaces, but just as he moves toward\n
Nosuri menacingly, he freezes in place.
Why, my good man. Whatever is the matter? Can't\n
you move?
U-Urgh...
The man called Ougi--at my side just a moment ago--\n
reappears behind Moznu, holding a thin blade to\n
his neck.
What's with this guy? I didn't even see him move...
H-Hey now, I'm just joking, yeah? All in g-good\n
fun. Take it easy.
It's all right, Ougi. Forgiving a man for his\n
rudeness is what a good woman does, after all.
If you insist, dearest sister, then I defer to\n
your judgement.
And just like that, Ougi slips his blade back\n
into his sleeve and walks away as if nothing\n
happened at all.
Ghh... Guh. Geh heh. Heh! Whatever. I never\n
liked huge tits anyway!
Spitting a weak parting shot over his shoulder\n
like a sore loser, Moznu turns and runs...
They're gonna shrivel up and droop when you're an\n
old hag, y'know!
--and gives a final insult as he disappears into\n
the thicket.
Is something the matter, sister?
Ah?
...It's nothing. Carry on.
Ougi waves to the remaining men at her command,\n
who gather around the caravan's unmanned wagons...
Anything loaded with cargo--even that strange,\n
covered one--is hauled off in short order.
Please forgive the inconvenience. We'll be taking\n
this. Farewell!
Do take care on the road, friends. Until we meet\n
again.
With bizarrely polite farewells, both of them\n
disappear with the last of the bandits into the\n
woods.
...Good grief. Well, at least we survived...
I mean. We're tied up deep in the mountains, sure,\n
but we survived. Now how are we supposed to break\n
out of this!?
*Wriggle, wriggle--*
No sooner than the thought crosses my mind,\n
I notice Kuon, wiggling her body oddly behind me.
There.
*Flumph*...
The ropes tightly wrapped around her suddenly\n
fall to the ground.
A rope escape, huh? You're gonna have to teach me\n
that if this turns into a regular thing.
Mm, there's a little trick to it, that's all. You\n
just dislocate your fingers.
I'm, uh, reasonably sure that's a pretty big leap\n
from a "little trick."
Sure it is. Just hold still and let me untie you.
Kuon makes her way down the line, starting with\n
me, then untying everyone in quick succession.
A-Ah, thank you...
Hey, missy. Do mine, too, won't you?
Oh? I don't think I need to untie yours.
What're you talking about?
You're a pretty good actor, Ukon, but enough of\n
the charade. I could tell something was off.
You weren't being cautious at all, what with a\n
suspicious woman approaching us unsolicited.
Hm.
It's not like you at all. A mercenary captain,\n
letting his guard down? I hardly think so.
You're overestimating me, I think, missy.
And the way you took her story at face value was\n
unlike you, too. You would've asked for details.
Ngh...
Moreover, after they surrounded us, only Rulutieh\n
and Maroro were upset. Everyone else stayed calm.
With all of that in mind, that was a strange\n
encounter we just had, wouldn't you agree?
...So that's why Kuon was so unflappable. She\n
noticed something was up.
Had it been one or two "off" notes I might have\n
let it slide, but the whole thing feels too\n
suspicious.
Cripes. All right, already. I give. You've got\n
some scary deductive abilities, y'know that?
You'll explain, won't you?
Ukon smiles wryly, then stands without issue.
The ropes supposedly "tying him up" flutter\n
uselessly to the ground at his feet.
Wh-What bewitcheth yon ropes!?
Ah, but first--How'd it go, you two?
The twins nod, both silently pointing off in the\n
same direction.
Hm? When did they--
All right, then. Looks like everything's ready.
I guess I should make the big reveal, then...
Ukon scratches the back of his head, turning back\n
toward us.
Well, first. Remember how I said no bandit would\n
dare attack us? I, uh, might've lied a little bit.
Hey!
See, this area's been plagued by more and more\n
bandits and highway robberies like this recently.
Even way out here in Kujyuri, efforts to bring 'em\n
in haven't exactly gone well.
Seems the bandits in question have been moving\n
their stronghold regularly, throwing off the hunt.
And all the while, they've been raiding and\n
robbing, scaling up, recruiting new members...
They can't just be left to their own devices, at\n
this point. We were asked to take care of 'em.
Ukon looks over to Rulutieh apologetically.
E-Eh?
Sorry for putting you through this mess, princess.\n
We thought it'd be better if you didn't know.
I-I see... So... that's why...
M-Master Ukon! Prithee, speak truth! I knew naught\n
of these plans.
Yyyeah, about that. Maroro, you're not exactly the\n
best actor in the world. Had to minimize risks.
I... see...
C'mon, man, it's not like I made the decision\n
myself. It was a group thing, we a--men? Men,\n
look at me!
Well, Maroro's kinda... Yeah. I guess I can see\n
that. But hey, doesn't that mean this was a tr--
Urgh--
Wow, kid. Rude. But yes, this was a purposeful\n
trap. Faster to track 'em to their base this way.
Hold on, hold on. I get that, but what would you\n
have done if they'd actually ATTACKED us?
Thankfully nothing happened, but for a second\n
there, things were about to get pretty ugly.
The way that Moznu guy was leering at those two...
Rulutieh must've been scared out of her mind, with\n
her face buried in Cocopo's fluff like that.
Hm? Ah, yeah. About that.
So that's what was going on...
As Ukon tries to talk around her, Kuon cuts to the\n
heart of things.
Either there's a traitor in their ranks, or one of\n
ours infiltrated them. Am I right?
No wonder things seemed suspicious.
That's about the shape of it. We'll be joining up\n
with a company waiting nearby, then attacking\n
their base.
I know you've got more questions, and you deserve\n
answers, but save 'em. Every minute counts, now.
Oh, no you don't. How can you suggest that so\n
calmly?
Escorting cargo I signed on for, but launching\n
surprise attacks on bandit fortresses? C'mon,\n
give me a break.
Yeah, I know. I'm not planning on bringing you\n
along for this. Relax.
Saying that, Ukon smiles.
Instead... I hate to ask this of you, but I need\n
a tiny favor.
